United States|Australia.headline: USMNT cruise past Australia in Seattle
United States|Australia.summary: An early Cameron Burgess own goal settled the hosts before Alex Freeman doubled the lead before half-time. The United States controlled the contest and saw out a comfortable 2-0 win to open their account.
United States|Australia.star_reason: Capped a lively display with the decisive second goal on the stroke of half-time.
United States|Australia.turning_point: Burgess's 11th-minute own goal handed the USMNT an early grip on the game.
United States|Australia.what_lacked: Australia created little of note and never tested the US back line.

Scotland|Morocco.headline: Saibari's record-quick strike sinks Scotland
Scotland|Morocco.summary: Ismael Saibari scored after just 72 seconds — the fastest goal of the tournament — and Morocco defended resolutely to hold on for a 1-0 win. Scotland pushed but were denied, with a McTominay penalty appeal waved away.
Scotland|Morocco.star_reason: His 2nd-minute strike was the only goal and set the tone for Morocco's disciplined display.
Scotland|Morocco.turning_point: Brahim Diaz's lofted ball released Saibari inside two minutes.
Scotland|Morocco.what_lacked: Scotland lacked a cutting edge in the final third and missed their chance to qualify.

Brazil|Haiti.headline: Cunha double powers Brazil past Haiti
Brazil|Haiti.summary: Matheus Cunha struck twice in the first half and Vinicius Junior added a third on the stroke of half-time as Brazil eliminated Haiti with a convincing 3-0 win in Philadelphia. Neymar was rested.
Brazil|Haiti.star_reason: Two first-half goals (23', 36') broke the game open and reawakened Selecao hopes.
Brazil|Haiti.turning_point: Cunha's quickfire brace put Brazil in command before the interval.
Brazil|Haiti.what_lacked: Haiti were organised but offered almost no attacking threat against a rotated Brazil.

Paraguay|Turkey.headline: Galarza's early bolt stuns Turkey
Paraguay|Turkey.summary: Matias Galarza scored inside 65 seconds and Paraguay held on — even after Miguel Almiron's first-half red card left them a man down — to beat Turkey 1-0 and eliminate the Turks. Galarza's effort squeezed through Merih Demiral and beat Ugurcan Cakir.
Paraguay|Turkey.star_reason: His sub-2-minute winner was the difference and set a tournament goal record.
Paraguay|Turkey.turning_point: Almiron's straight red after a VAR review forced Paraguay to defend for over an hour.
Paraguay|Turkey.what_lacked: Turkey dominated the ball but could not break down ten-man Paraguay.

Netherlands|Sweden.headline: Dutch firepower overwhelms Sweden
Netherlands|Sweden.summary: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo each scored twice and Crysencio Summerville added a late fifth as the Netherlands raced to a 5-1 win and top of Group F. Anthony Elanga grabbed a consolation for Sweden.
Netherlands|Sweden.star_reason: Two goals in seven second-half minutes (47', 54') put the result beyond doubt.
Netherlands|Sweden.turning_point: Brobbey's 5th-minute opener set an early tone Sweden could never recover from.
Netherlands|Sweden.what_lacked: Sweden had no answer to the Dutch transition and were overrun in midfield.

Germany|Ivory Coast.headline: Undav off the bench rescues Germany
Germany|Ivory Coast.summary: Franck Kessie put Ivory Coast ahead at the break, but substitute Deniz Undav levelled in the 68th minute and snatched a 94th-minute winner to send Germany into the Round of 32 with a dramatic 2-1 comeback.
Germany|Ivory Coast.star_reason: A match-changing brace off the bench, including the stoppage-time winner.
Germany|Ivory Coast.turning_point: Nagelsmann's substitutions (Undav, Amiri, Nmecha) flipped a flat first-half display.
Germany|Ivory Coast.what_lacked: Ivory Coast led at half-time but couldn't manage the game and conceded twice late.

Ecuador|Curaçao.headline: Room's heroics earn Curacao a historic point
Ecuador|Curaçao.summary: Goalkeeper Eloy Room made 15 saves as Curacao held Ecuador to a 0-0 draw for their first-ever World Cup point. Ecuador's 15 shots on target without scoring was the most in a World Cup match since 1966.
Ecuador|Curaçao.star_reason: Fifteen saves — a one-man wall that denied a dominant Ecuador all night.
Ecuador|Curaçao.turning_point: Enner Valencia's two gilt-edged misses summed up Ecuador's frustration.
Ecuador|Curaçao.what_lacked: Ecuador dominated possession and chances but lacked a finishing touch.

Tunisia|Japan.headline: Ueda double sends Japan top, eliminates Tunisia
Tunisia|Japan.summary: Daichi Kamada scored Japan's fastest-ever World Cup goal in the 4th minute and Ayase Ueda struck twice, with Junya Ito also on target, as Japan thrashed Tunisia 4-0 — the first AFC side to score four in a World Cup game. Tunisia were eliminated.
Tunisia|Japan.star_reason: A first-half strike and a late header capped a clinical Japan performance.
Tunisia|Japan.turning_point: Kamada's 4th-minute opener set Japan on their way inside five minutes.
Tunisia|Japan.what_lacked: Tunisia were second best across the pitch and never threatened a route back.

Spain|Saudi Arabia.headline: Yamal and Oyarzabal run riot as Spain cruise past Saudi Arabia
Spain|Saudi Arabia.summary: Lamine Yamal opened inside 10 minutes and Mikel Oyarzabal struck twice in a three-minute spell to put Spain out of sight before half-time. A Hassan Al-Tambakti own goal early in the second half completed a comfortable 4-0 win that sends Spain top of Group H.
Spain|Saudi Arabia.star_reason: A clinical 21st- and 24th-minute brace turned an early lead into a rout.
Spain|Saudi Arabia.turning_point: Oyarzabal's quickfire double midway through the first half killed the contest.
Spain|Saudi Arabia.what_lacked: Saudi Arabia offered little going forward and were undone by Spain’s movement between the lines.

Belgium|Iran.headline: Ten-man Belgium held by stubborn Iran in goalless stalemate
Belgium|Iran.summary: Belgium were frustrated by a disciplined Iran and saw their afternoon unravel when they were reduced to ten men early in the second half. Iran defended deep and dug in for a 0-0 draw that leaves Group G wide open ahead of New Zealand vs Egypt.
Belgium|Iran.star_reason: A compact, organised back line shut out one of the tournament favourites.
Belgium|Iran.turning_point: Belgium's second-half red card forced them onto the back foot.
Belgium|Iran.what_lacked: Belgium lacked a cutting edge in the final third and paid for the dismissal.

Uruguay|Cape Verde.headline: Cape Verde stun Uruguay with late leveller in 2-2 thriller
Uruguay|Cape Verde.summary: Kevin Pina’s 21st-minute free-kick put Cape Verde ahead before Uruguay roared back through Maximiliano Araújo and an Agustín Canobbio strike in first-half stoppage time. Helio Varela’s 61st-minute equaliser earned the Blue Sharks a shock 2-2 draw with La Celeste.
Uruguay|Cape Verde.star_reason: His second-half equaliser snatched a historic point for Cape Verde.
Uruguay|Cape Verde.turning_point: Varela's 61st-minute strike cancelled out Uruguay's late first-half turnaround.
Uruguay|Cape Verde.what_lacked: Uruguay switched off defensively after the break and could not see out a winning position.

Colombia|DR Congo.headline: Muñoz header sends Colombia into the knockouts
Colombia|DR Congo.summary: Daniel Muñoz's 76th-minute strike settled a tense Group K clash in Guadalajara, giving Colombia a 1-0 win over DR Congo and sealing their place in the last 32 with a game to spare.
Colombia|DR Congo.star_reason: The wing-back's late goal broke a stubborn Congolese resistance and booked Colombia's knockout berth.
Colombia|DR Congo.turning_point: Muñoz's 76th-minute finish after Colombia had been frustrated for over an hour.
Colombia|DR Congo.what_lacked: DR Congo defended well but offered too little going forward and are now on the brink of elimination.

England|Ghana.headline: Ghana frustrate England in goalless stalemate
England|Ghana.summary: England extended their unbeaten start but were held to a 0-0 draw by a disciplined Ghana side in Boston. Nico O'Reilly and Harry Kane spurned late chances as the Black Stars earned a battling point.
England|Ghana.star_reason: A resolute rearguard kept England's attack at bay for the full 90 minutes.
England|Ghana.turning_point: Kane's late missed opening that would have spared England's blushes.
England|Ghana.what_lacked: England lacked a cutting edge in the final third and could not break a packed Ghanaian block.

Panama|Croatia.headline: Budimir strike downs Panama as Croatia revive hopes
Panama|Croatia.summary: Ante Budimir's 54th-minute goal gave Croatia a crucial 1-0 win over Panama in Toronto. A second straight 1-0 defeat eliminates Panama, while the Vatreni keep their knockout ambitions alive.
Panama|Croatia.star_reason: His second-half finish delivered the win Croatia desperately needed.
Panama|Croatia.turning_point: Budimir's 54th-minute strike in a cagey, low-chance affair.
Panama|Croatia.what_lacked: Panama again failed to score and pay the price, exiting the tournament after two 1-0 losses.

Switzerland|Canada.headline: Switzerland edge hosts Canada to seal top spot
Switzerland|Canada.summary: Rubén Vargas struck right after the break and Johan Manzambi added a second on 57' as Switzerland came from a goalless first half to beat Canada 2-1 in Vancouver. Promise David's late header set up a nervy finish but the Swiss held on.
Switzerland|Canada.star_reason: His 57th-minute goal proved the decisive blow in a tight Group B decider.
Switzerland|Canada.turning_point: Vargas breaking the deadlock seconds into the second half.
Switzerland|Canada.what_lacked: Canada left it too late to respond and could not force an equaliser on home soil.

Bosnia and Herzegovina|Qatar.headline: Teenager Alajbegović inspires Bosnia rout of Qatar
Bosnia and Herzegovina|Qatar.summary: 18-year-old Kerim Alajbegović fired Bosnia ahead with a stunning long-range strike before a Sultan Al-Brake own goal doubled the lead. Hassan Al-Haydos pulled one back for Qatar but Ermin Mahmić's late goal sealed a 3-1 win that boosts Bosnia's knockout hopes and eliminates Qatar.
Bosnia and Herzegovina|Qatar.star_reason: His thunderous opener set Bosnia on their way in a standout teenage display.
Bosnia and Herzegovina|Qatar.what_lacked: Qatar's defending under pressure unravelled and they exit the tournament.

Scotland|Brazil.headline: Vinícius double sends Brazil top of Group C
Scotland|Brazil.summary: Vinícius Júnior was unplayable, striking inside seven minutes after a McKenna error and heading home just before the break. Matheus Cunha completed a 3-0 win that confirmed Brazil as Group C winners, dominating with 4.46 xG to Scotland's 1.13.
Scotland|Brazil.star_reason: A devastating two-goal display tore Scotland apart.
Scotland|Brazil.turning_point: Vinícius' 7th-minute opener that set the tone.
Scotland|Brazil.what_lacked: Scotland's early mistakes gifted Brazil control and they never recovered.

Morocco|Haiti.headline: Morocco survive Haiti scare in six-goal thriller
Morocco|Haiti.summary: An early Bounou own goal gave Haiti a shock lead but Morocco roared back through Achraf Hakimi and Ismael Saibari before half-time. Wilson Isidor kept Haiti in it, yet Soufiane Rahimi and Gessime Yassine sealed a chaotic 4-2 win that took Morocco second in Group C.
Morocco|Haiti.star_reason: His equaliser swung momentum back to Morocco in a frantic first half.
Morocco|Haiti.turning_point: Saibari's stoppage-time goal to make it 2-1 before the break.
Morocco|Haiti.what_lacked: Morocco's defending was loose throughout; Haiti bow out after a brave show.

South Africa|South Korea.headline: Maseko sends South Africa into knockouts for the first time
South Africa|South Korea.summary: Thapelo Maseko's 63rd-minute strike, set up by Tshepang Moremi, gave South Africa a 1-0 win over South Korea and booked Bafana Bafana's place in the World Cup knockout stage for the first time in their history.
South Africa|South Korea.star_reason: His clinical second-half finish settled a tense Group A decider.
South Africa|South Korea.turning_point: Maseko beating Kim Seung-gyu with a low strike into the bottom corner on 63'.
South Africa|South Korea.what_lacked: South Korea, who dropped captain Son Heung-min, lacked a cutting edge and exit the tournament.

Czech Republic|Mexico.headline: Mexico cruise past Czechia to win Group A
Czech Republic|Mexico.summary: Mateo Chávez broke the deadlock after the hour before Julián Quiñones and a stoppage-time Álvaro Fidalgo strike completed a 3-0 win over Czechia in Mexico City, confirming El Tri as Group A winners.
Czech Republic|Mexico.star_reason: His 55th-minute opener unlocked a stubborn Czech defence.
Czech Republic|Mexico.turning_point: Chávez's goal that finally broke through after a goalless first half.
Czech Republic|Mexico.what_lacked: Czechia offered little going forward and exit the World Cup.

Ecuador|Germany.headline: Plata's late winner sends Ecuador through as Germany bow out on top
Ecuador|Germany.summary: Ecuador produced a stunning fightback to beat Germany 2-1 at MetLife Stadium and advance as one of the best third-placed sides. Leroy Sané's early opener was cancelled by Nilson Angulo nine minutes in, before Gonzalo Plata stabbed home the winner with 13 minutes left. Germany, already qualified as Group E winners, finished top despite the loss.
Ecuador|Germany.star_reason: His 77th-minute close-range finish completed Ecuador's comeback and secured their historic knockout berth.
Ecuador|Germany.turning_point: Angulo's rapid equaliser on 9 minutes settled Ecuador's nerves and swung the momentum away from Germany.
Ecuador|Germany.what_lacked: Germany lacked urgency with qualification already secured, allowing Ecuador to exploit space in behind.

Curaçao|Ivory Coast.headline: Pépé brace powers Ivory Coast into knockouts for first time
Curaçao|Ivory Coast.summary: Nicolas Pépé scored twice as Ivory Coast defeated Curaçao 2-0 in Philadelphia to qualify for the World Cup knockout stages for the first time. The former Arsenal forward capitalised on a defensive error to open the scoring after just seven minutes, then coolly added a second to book Les Éléphants' historic place in the last 32.
Curaçao|Ivory Coast.star_reason: A two-goal masterclass — clinical finish at 7' and a composed second — made Ivory Coast's first knockout qualification a reality.
Curaçao|Ivory Coast.turning_point: Pépé's opener in the 7th minute, punishing a Curaçao backline mistake from a Yan Diomandé cross.
Curaçao|Ivory Coast.what_lacked: Curaçao offered little threat going forward and were unable to recover after conceding so early.

Japan|Sweden.headline: Elanga rescues Sweden as both sides advance from Group F
Japan|Sweden.summary: Japan and Sweden played out a 1-1 draw at AT&T Stadium, with both teams advancing to the knockout stage. Daizen Maeda's composed finish from a Ritsu Doan through-ball gave Japan the lead in the 56th minute, but Anthony Elanga's left-footed cross-shot six minutes later earned Sweden a point and second place in Group F.
Japan|Sweden.star_reason: His 62nd-minute equaliser — his second goal of the tournament — rescued Sweden's second-place finish and denied Japan a group win.
Japan|Sweden.turning_point: Maeda's opener threatened to push Sweden into a tighter bracket path, but Elanga's immediate response restored parity.
Japan|Sweden.what_lacked: Japan failed to protect their lead after the break and could not hold off Sweden's pressing response.

Tunisia|Netherlands.headline: Brobbey and two own goals seal Group F top spot for Netherlands
Tunisia|Netherlands.summary: The Netherlands topped Group F with a comfortable 3-1 win over Tunisia in Kansas City. Ellys Skhiri's own goal on 3 minutes and Brian Brobbey's strike four minutes later gave the Dutch a flying start. Tunisia briefly halved the deficit through Hazem Mastouri (54'), but Anis Slimane's own goal in the 62nd minute ended resistance.
Tunisia|Netherlands.star_reason: His third goal of the tournament gave Netherlands daylight after two minutes and underlined their dominance throughout the group stage.
Tunisia|Netherlands.turning_point: Brobbey's second-minute goal compounded the opening own goal, leaving Tunisia needing a miracle they never produced.
Tunisia|Netherlands.what_lacked: Tunisia's defensive shape crumbled from the first whistle and their consolation came too late to matter.

Turkey|United States.headline: Ayhan's 98th-minute winner hands Turkey comeback victory over US
Turkey|United States.summary: Kaan Ayhan stabbed home in the 98th minute as Turkey stunned the United States 3-2 in Los Angeles, despite the USMNT already winning Group D. Auston Trusty's third-minute header put the US ahead, but Arda Güler equalised and Orkun Kökcü put Turkey in front. Sebastian Berhalter levelled at 2-2 after the break, before Ayhan's dramatic late winner denied a US squad of rotated players a clean sheet.
Turkey|United States.star_reason: Calmly levelled after the US opener and orchestrated Turkey's midfield throughout, setting the platform for the comeback.
Turkey|United States.turning_point: Kökcü's 31st-minute goal gave Turkey a lead they briefly surrendered, but Ayhan's stoppage-time strike was the defining moment.
Turkey|United States.what_lacked: The US fielded heavily rotated players with qualification secure, and were unable to clear their lines in the final seconds.

Paraguay|Australia.headline: Goalless draw enough for Paraguay and Australia to both advance
Paraguay|Australia.summary: A cautious, goalless draw in San Francisco saw both Paraguay and Australia advance to the knockout round. With the US having already topped Group D, both sides needed only to avoid heavy defeat. The game was largely without incident — Jordan Bos had Australia's best chance in the 90th minute — and Diego Gómez's second yellow card of the group stage rules him out of the round of 32.
Paraguay|Australia.star_reason: The Paraguay midfielder was composed under pressure and helped marshal a disciplined defensive effort throughout.
Paraguay|Australia.turning_point: The scoreless first half set the cautious tone; neither side was willing to risk conceding in a game that suited both.
Paraguay|Australia.what_lacked: Both teams lacked attacking ambition, content with a draw that guaranteed safe passage to the knockouts.

Norway|France.headline: Dembele hat-trick fires France to perfect group stage
Norway|France.summary: Ousmane Dembele terrorised the Norwegian defence all afternoon, completing the second-fastest hat-trick in World Cup history by the 32nd minute. Thelo Aasgaard's consolation sandwiched between the second and third Dembele goals briefly gave the Norwegians hope, but Desire Doue swept in a fourth in stoppage time to seal France's third straight group win.
Norway|France.star_reason: Hat-trick in 32 minutes — only Erich Probst (Austria, 1954) has been faster at a World Cup.
Norway|France.turning_point: Dembele's 7th-minute opener put France in control from the off and Norway never recovered.
Norway|France.what_lacked: Norway had the better xG (1.69 to France's 1.31) but finishing was poor and a missed spot-kick proved costly.

Senegal|Iraq.headline: Senegal demolish 10-man Iraq to reach Round of 32
Senegal|Iraq.summary: Habib Diarra struck inside four minutes to settle Senegal's nerves, and a VAR-upgraded red card for Iraq's Rebin Sulaka reduced the contest to a formality. Ismaila Sarr doubled the lead after the break before Pape Gueye's clinical brace and Iliman Ndiaye's long-range finish completed a 5-0 rout that sent the Lions of Teranga into the knockout rounds.
Senegal|Iraq.star_reason: Two goals and a commanding midfield display drove the second-half blitz that put the tie beyond doubt.
Senegal|Iraq.turning_point: Sulaka's red card on the stroke of half-time effectively ended Iraq as a contest.
Senegal|Iraq.what_lacked: Iraq were already stretched before the red card and offered almost nothing going forward throughout.

England|DR Congo.headline: Kane's late brace rescues England against DR Congo
England|DR Congo.summary: Brian Cipenga's seventh-minute strike looked set to cause a stunning upset, but Harry Kane delivered twice in the final 15 minutes to complete a 2-1 comeback. Gordon's crossing was pivotal — he created both Kane goals as England advance to face Mexico in the Round of 16.
England|DR Congo.star_reason: Two goals in four minutes (75', 86') — both headers/strikes — turned the game on its head after England trailed for 68 minutes.
England|DR Congo.turning_point: Gordon's 75th-minute cross gave Kane a chance to equalise and lift a nervy England side.
England|DR Congo.what_lacked: England were sluggish for much of the match and allowed DR Congo to sit deep and threaten on the counter.

Belgium|Senegal.headline: Tielemans' 120+5 penalty completes Belgium's miracle comeback
Belgium|Senegal.summary: Senegal led 2-0 with four minutes of normal time remaining through Habib Diarra and Ismaïla Sarr. Romelu Lukaku pulled one back in the 86th minute before Tielemans headed home in the 89th to force extra time. A controversial VAR-awarded penalty in the dying moments of extra time allowed Tielemans to convert at 120+5 — the latest winning goal in World Cup history.
Belgium|Senegal.star_reason: Two crucial goals in the comeback (89', 120+5 pen) — his extra-time penalty was the decisive blow.
Belgium|Senegal.turning_point: Lukaku's 86th-minute goal sparked a frantic finale that Senegal could not survive.
Belgium|Senegal.what_lacked: Senegal were comfortable at 2-0 but couldn't defend their lead in the final minutes — poor game management cost them.

United States|Bosnia and Herzegovina.headline: 10-man USMNT beat Bosnia for first knockout win since 2002
United States|Bosnia and Herzegovina.summary: Folarin Balogun nutmegged Vasilj just before half-time to give the hosts the lead, but was then controversially sent off in the 64th minute for a VAR-reviewed tackle. Despite playing with 10 men, Malik Tillman curled a stunning free kick into the top corner in the 82nd minute to seal a 2-0 win — the USMNT's first World Cup knockout victory in 24 years.
United States|Bosnia and Herzegovina.star_reason: His 82nd-minute free kick doubled the lead while the US were a man down and put the tie beyond doubt.
United States|Bosnia and Herzegovina.turning_point: Tillman's free kick after Balogun's red card swung the momentum firmly back to the hosts.
United States|Bosnia and Herzegovina.what_lacked: Bosnia struggled to create chances against a disciplined US defence and never threatened after going behind.

Canada|Morocco.headline: Rahimi seals it as Morocco end Canada's historic run
Canada|Morocco.summary: Ismael Saibari's night ended after 21 minutes when he went down injured, and his replacement Soufiane Rahimi had the last word. Azzedine Ounahi scored either side of the break — a curling free-kick in the 50th minute and a first-time finish from a Brahim Diaz through-ball in the 82nd — before Rahimi added a third in stoppage time, again teed up by Diaz, whose two assists gave him a tournament-record four. Canada created chances of their own (10 shots to Morocco's 5) but could not beat Yassine Bounou, and their first-ever World Cup knockout run ends in the Round of 16.
Canada|Morocco.star_reason: Set up both of Morocco's second-half goals, taking his tournament assist tally to four — an African record at a single World Cup.
Canada|Morocco.turning_point: Saibari's 21st-minute injury brought on Rahimi, who scored the clinching third goal deep into stoppage time.
Canada|Morocco.what_lacked: Canada had the better of the shot count but couldn't turn pressure into clear-cut chances against a well-organised Morocco defence.

Paraguay|France.headline: Mbappé penalty is enough as France survive Paraguay's heat test
Paraguay|France.summary: France dominated possession (76%) and shots (15 to 5) but were held scoreless until the 70th minute, when Kylian Mbappé converted a penalty won by substitute Désiré Doué and confirmed by VAR. Paraguay, playing a disciplined, physical game in punishing Philadelphia heat, had no reply — goalkeeper Orlando Gill kept the margin at one with a string of second-half saves, including a stoppage-time double stop to deny Mbappé a second. The goal was Mbappé's 19th at a World Cup, drawing him level with Messi atop the Golden Boot on 7, and sends France into the quarter-finals to face Morocco.
Paraguay|France.star_reason: His 70th-minute penalty was the only goal of a stifling, tightly-fought game — his 7th of the tournament, tying Messi for the Golden Boot lead.
Paraguay|France.turning_point: Doué's introduction at 61' immediately paid off — he drew the foul that led to Mbappé's match-winning penalty.
Paraguay|France.what_lacked: Paraguay defended resolutely but managed just one shot on target and rarely threatened going forward.

Brazil|Norway.headline: Haaland, Norway condemn Brazil to earliest World Cup exit since 1990
Brazil|Norway.summary: Brazil were wasteful throughout — Bruno Guimarães had a first-half penalty saved by Ørjan Nyland after a VAR review — and paid the price late on. Substitute Andreas Schjelderup, on at the break, set up both Erling Haaland goals either side of the 80th minute: a cross turned home in the 79th, then a low, driven finish in the 90th. Neymar's stoppage-time penalty, won after an elbow on Casemiro, was too little too late. The result marks Brazil's earliest World Cup exit since 1990 and, by his own announcement afterward, Neymar's final appearance for the Seleção. Norway reach the quarter-finals for the first time in their history.
Brazil|Norway.star_reason: Scored twice in the final 11 minutes to send Norway through — his 7th goal of the tournament draws him level with Messi and Mbappé atop the Golden Boot.
Brazil|Norway.turning_point: Schjelderup's half-time introduction — he set up both of Haaland's goals.
Brazil|Norway.what_lacked: Brazil created chances (including a missed first-half penalty) but were toothless in front of goal until a consolation penalty in stoppage time.

Mexico|England.headline: Bellingham's quickfire double and Kane's penalty send England past 10-man scare at the Azteca
Mexico|England.summary: Jude Bellingham scored twice in 98 seconds either side of the half-hour to give England control at a raucous Azteca, before Julián Quiñones halved the gap just before the break. Jarell Quansah was sent off in the 54th minute for a high challenge on Jesús Gallardo, but England extended their lead from the spot through Harry Kane minutes later. Raúl Jiménez's own penalty, in the 69th, set up a grandstand finish, and Mexico threw everything at a 10-man England for the final 21 minutes plus 11 of stoppage time — but Jordan Pickford and the England defence held out. England become the first team ever to beat Mexico in a World Cup match at the Azteca.
Mexico|England.star_reason: Scored twice in 98 seconds to set the tone, calling it the best night of his England career.
Mexico|England.turning_point: Quansah's 54th-minute red card should have swung the game toward Mexico, but Kane's penalty minutes later restored England's two-goal cushion instead.
Mexico|England.what_lacked: Mexico had a full second half plus stoppage time with a man advantage but couldn't find a fourth English error or a decisive equaliser.

Portugal|Spain.headline: Substitute Merino breaks Portuguese hearts in the 91st minute as Ronaldo's World Cup career ends
Portugal|Spain.summary: A tense, largely chanceless Round of 16 tie in the Dallas-area heat was settled in the first minute of stoppage time: substitute Mikel Merino took a pass from fellow substitute Ferran Torres on the edge of the box and finished low into the corner. Portugal's best chance had come earlier through Nuno Mendes, whose deflected effort came back off the crossbar, but they never found a way through Spain's midfield, marshalled all game by Man of the Match Rodri. The result ends Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career — he had confirmed before the tournament this would be his last — and prompted Portugal manager Roberto Martínez to resign on the spot. Spain advance to face Belgium in the quarter-finals.
Portugal|Spain.star_reason: Controlled Spain's midfield for the full 90+ minutes and set up the platform for the two late substitutes to combine for the winner.
Portugal|Spain.turning_point: Spain's double substitution just before the hour — Merino and Torres both coming on — directly produced the 91st-minute winner.
Portugal|Spain.what_lacked: Portugal created little beyond Nuno Mendes's deflected crossbar effort and never tested Unai Simón in the second half.

United States|Belgium.headline: De Ketelaere brace sends Belgium past the hosts as the USA become the last co-host eliminated
United States|Belgium.summary: Charles De Ketelaere scored twice inside the first half-hour — either side of Malik Tillman's leveller for the hosts — as Belgium controlled the game from start to finish at Lumen Field. Hans Vanaken, on early for the injured Amadou Onana, made it 3-1 just after the hour, and substitute Romelu Lukaku added a fourth in stoppage time to complete a comfortable win. The United States never recovered from De Ketelaere's early opener and become the last of the three co-hosts eliminated, following Canada and Mexico. Belgium advance to face Spain in the quarter-finals.
United States|Belgium.star_reason: His brace inside the first half-hour set the tone and gave Belgium a lead they never relinquished.
United States|Belgium.turning_point: De Ketelaere's 9th-minute opener — Tillman's leveller 22 minutes later was as close as the USA got before Belgium pulled away.
United States|Belgium.what_lacked: The hosts had no answer defensively once Onana went off injured; Vanaken and Lukaku both scored off the bench in the space they were given.

Argentina|Egypt.headline: Messi and Argentina erupt for three goals in 13 minutes to complete a stunning comeback past Egypt
Argentina|Egypt.summary: Egypt had Argentina on the ropes in Atlanta — Yasser Ibrahim's early header and a Mostafa Zico strike sandwiched a save by Mostafa Shoubir off a first-half Messi penalty, leaving the reigning champions 2-0 down with 20 minutes to go. Then the collapse: Cristian Romero headed in from a Messi cross in the 79th minute, Messi leveled it himself four minutes later, and Enzo Fernández rose to head home a stoppage-time winner in the 92nd. Argentina dominated the underlying numbers throughout (2.80 xG to 0.98, 64% possession) but needed a furious late rally to turn control into a result, extending Messi's scoring streak and sending Argentina through to face the winner of Switzerland vs Colombia.
Argentina|Egypt.star_reason: Missed a first-half penalty but kept probing, then set up Romero's equalizer before scoring the leveler himself in a decisive four-minute spell.
Argentina|Egypt.turning_point: Cristian Romero's 79th-minute header off a Messi cross broke the deadlock and ignited the comeback.
Argentina|Egypt.what_lacked: Egypt sat too deep after going 2-0 up and couldn't manage the game's closing stages, conceding three times in the final 13 minutes.

Switzerland|Colombia.headline: Kobel's shootout heroics send Switzerland past a dominant Colombia and into the last eight for the first time since 1954
Switzerland|Colombia.summary: Colombia had the better of a tense, goalless 120 minutes in Vancouver — Jhon Lucumí's header cannoned back off the crossbar and Jaminton Campaz spurned a clear one-on-one during extra time — but couldn't find a breakthrough against a resolute Swiss backline. The shootout swung on goalkeeper Gregor Kobel's save from Cucho Hernández, and Rubén Vargas made no mistake with the winning kick, sending Camilo Vargas the wrong way to seal it 4-3 on penalties. Switzerland reach the World Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954 and will face Argentina next.
Switzerland|Colombia.star_reason: Marshalled a defense that survived a 1.03-to-0.35 xG deficit over 120 minutes, then produced the decisive save in the shootout.
Switzerland|Colombia.turning_point: Kobel's save from Cucho Hernández in the shootout — Switzerland never trailed in the kicks after that.
Switzerland|Colombia.what_lacked: Colombia created the better chances (Lucumí's crossbar header, Campaz's spurned one-on-one; 1.03 xG to Switzerland's 0.35) but converted just 3 of 7 shots on target, unable to turn territory into a goal; Switzerland, by contrast, managed only 1 shot on target from 22 attempts.

France|Morocco.headline: Mbappé makes amends for a missed penalty as France ease past Morocco and into the semi-finals
France|Morocco.summary: Kylian Mbappé saw a first-half penalty saved by Yassine Bounou after Didier Deschamps left him waiting to take it, but made up for it just after the hour with a curling finish into the far corner, and Ousmane Dembélé doubled the lead six minutes later with a low drive. France dominated the underlying numbers (3.69 xG to 0.14) despite Morocco holding more of the ball, ending the last African side's run in the tournament and reaching a third straight World Cup semi-final — a feat only Brazil and Germany have managed. Mbappé took a knock on the ankle late on but allayed any injury concern.
France|Morocco.star_reason: Missed a penalty, then scored the opener and set up the tone for Dembélé's second — his eighth goal of the tournament, level with Messi.
France|Morocco.turning_point: Mbappé's 60th-minute curler, breaking the deadlock right after his earlier spot-kick miss.
France|Morocco.what_lacked: Morocco held 52% possession but managed just 1 shot on target and 0.14 xG, unable to trouble Maignan's goal after Bounou's heroics from the penalty spot.

Spain|Belgium.headline: Merino's late strike sends Spain past Belgium and into the semi-finals
Spain|Belgium.summary: Fabián Ruiz put Spain ahead with a crisp finish from the edge of the box in the 30th minute, but Charles De Ketelaere levelled for Belgium right before half-time to set up a nervy second half. Mikel Merino settled it two minutes from time, pouncing after more incisive work from Lamine Yamal, who was again the architect of Spain's attacking play. Spain reach a fourth World Cup semi-final, where they'll face France.
Spain|Belgium.star_reason: Drove Spain's attack throughout and was directly involved in the buildup to both goals, named Man of the Match.
Spain|Belgium.turning_point: Merino's 88th-minute winner, snatching it back for Spain after Belgium had clawed level.
Spain|Belgium.what_lacked: Belgium's De Ketelaere equaliser was their only real moment of quality; they managed just 4 shots on target to Spain's 6.

Norway|England.headline: Bellingham brace sends England past Norway and into the last four
Norway|England.summary: Andreas Schjelderup gave Norway a first-half lead that had them dreaming of a first World Cup semi-final since 1938, but Jude Bellingham dragged England level in first-half stoppage time and struck again early in extra time to complete the turnaround. Bellingham's second goal means England now have two players — himself and Harry Kane — with six or more goals in the tournament, a World Cup first. England advance to face Argentina in the semi-final.
Norway|England.star_reason: Scored twice, including the extra-time winner, to drag England through a match they trailed for most of normal time.
Norway|England.turning_point: Bellingham's stoppage-time equaliser right before half-time, which kept England in the tie.
Norway|England.what_lacked: Norway couldn't build on Schjelderup's early goal and managed only 3 shots on target across 120 minutes.

Argentina|Switzerland.headline: Alvarez and Lautaro see off battling Switzerland in extra time
Argentina|Switzerland.summary: Alexis Mac Allister's early strike put Argentina in control, but Dan Ndoye's second-half leveller for Switzerland threatened a shock. Julián Alvarez restored the lead in extra time and Lautaro Martínez wrapped it up in the 121st minute. The result ended Lionel Messi's run of scoring in nine consecutive World Cup matches, but Argentina march on to a semi-final against England.
Argentina|Switzerland.star_reason: Broke the deadlock in extra time with a well-taken finish, named Man of the Match after a tireless performance up front.
Argentina|Switzerland.turning_point: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal, restoring Argentina's lead after Switzerland had drawn level.
Argentina|Switzerland.what_lacked: Switzerland's Ndoye equaliser aside, they created little else, finishing with just 3 shots on target.

France|Spain.headline: Oyarzabal and Porro send Spain past France and into the final
France|Spain.summary: Mikel Oyarzabal converted a 22nd-minute penalty and Pedro Porro doubled the lead with a well-taken finish in the 58th, and Spain controlled the game from there, with Rodri and the midfield starving France's front line of service. Kylian Mbappe and France never found a way past Unai Simon. Spain reach their first World Cup final since 2010.
France|Spain.star_reason: Scored the decisive second goal and was a constant outlet down the right, named Man of the Match.
France|Spain.turning_point: Oyarzabal's 22nd-minute penalty, which forced France to chase the game against a Spain side content to sit on the ball.
France|Spain.what_lacked: France managed just 3 shots on target and never troubled Unai Simon after going behind, with Mbappe starved of service.

England|Argentina.headline: Messi conjures another late Argentina comeback to sink England
England|Argentina.summary: Anthony Gordon's 55th-minute strike had England on course for a first World Cup final since 1966, but Argentina turned the screw after falling behind. Enzo Fernandez leveled with a bent finish from distance in the 85th, and Messi teed up Lautaro Martinez for a stoppage-time header that completed the turnaround. Argentina reach a second straight final; England face France in the third-place playoff.
England|Argentina.star_reason: Assisted both Argentina goals, including the stoppage-time winner for Martinez, in what may be his final World Cup match.
England|Argentina.turning_point: Fernandez's 85th-minute equalizer, which broke England's resistance and set up Messi's late assist for Martinez.
England|Argentina.what_lacked: England managed just 2 shots on target and sat too deep after taking the lead, letting Argentina dominate territory and chances late on.
